learning_outcomes.description: Explain how mutations and chromosomal abnormalities arise, and relate these to the pathogenesis of diseases.
Apply the use of molecular markers in diagnostics and animal breeding, explain their underlying methodologies, and evaluate their strengths and limitations as a diagnostic tool.
Explain the screening and treatment options for genetic diseases and genetic technologies for animal improvement.
Design animal breeding plans for disease control and animal production, based on sound genetic principles.
Work together in small groups to read and critically analyze the scientific literature.

assessments.task_description:
Quiz 1 will be held as an in-person invigilated quiz and will cover lecture content from weeks 1 - 4, inclusive.This quiz will be a 40-minute Inspera platform quiz.

Quiz 2 will be held as an in-person invigilated quiz and will cover lecture content from weeks 5 - 11, inclusive. This quiz will be a 40-minute Inspera platform quiz.
assessments.submission_guidelines: This demo highlights the most common questions and features and is designed to help you navigate your assessment in Inspera more easily. You may undertake the demo as many times as you wish. Your attempts are not tracked, and no responses will be saved or go towards your final grade. Please note, it is the student’s responsibility to ensure your exam browser is up to date prior to exams. Contact the campus library AskUs team for further assistance.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Students will work in groups of three (or four) to design and film a genetics counselling scenario. Each group must research the genetic condition that is provided, work together to write a script for their scenario and create a video that depicts a domestic animal owner being counselled about a genetic condition's basis, inheritance and prognosis. The group must submit a 10-minute video, a single page of academic references that were used during the research and an assignment coversheet detailing their use of AI.

A list of manuscripts, groups and further assessment details will be provided during semester. Guidelines and marking criteria for this assignment are in the Blackboard Assessment section.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Upload your assessment through the submission point (or Turnitin) in Blackboard Ultra.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: The End of Semester exam will cover the content of the lectures, practicals and tutorials of the whole semester. The exam will encompass short essay and problem-solving questions based on genetic disease case studies, genetic research scenarios and animal breeding goals.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 4(27 Jul - 23 Aug)
learning_activities.topic:
Genetic Fundamentals and Genetic Testing

This lecture series explores genetic fundamentals of inheritance and mutation in animals, and the principles and technologies for genetic testing and other applications.
learning_activities.topic_description: Genetic Fundamentals and Genetic Testing This lecture series explores genetic fundamentals of inheritance and mutation in animals, and the principles and technologies for genetic testing and other applications.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 2 To Week 6(03 Aug - 06 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Genetic Diseases and Population Genetics

This lecture series introduces some of the applications of genetics in the study of genetic diseases. We will explore the genetic processes affecting populations, including wildlife populations, and the use of genetic markers in individuals and populations.
learning_activities.topic_description: Genetic Diseases and Population Genetics This lecture series introduces some of the applications of genetics in the study of genetic diseases. We will explore the genetic processes affecting populations, including wildlife populations, and the use of genetic markers in individuals and populations.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 12(17 Aug - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Genetic Testing Consultation Scenario

Practical, self-directed learning and tutorial activities will provide an opportunity for students to explore the hands on laboratory techniques applicable to the isolation of genetic material and diagnosis of genetic diseases and the knowledge and skills to analyse their results.
learning_activities.topic_description: Genetic Testing Consultation Scenario Practical, self-directed learning and tutorial activities will provide an opportunity for students to explore the hands on laboratory techniques applicable to the isolation of genetic material and diagnosis of genetic diseases and the knowledge and skills to analyse their results.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 7(17 Aug - 13 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Population Genetics Tutorials

A series of small group tutorials and larger group calculation practice sessions will provide opportunities for students to apply lecture content to a diverse range of genetic disease and population scenarios.
learning_activities.topic_description: Population Genetics Tutorials A series of small group tutorials and larger group calculation practice sessions will provide opportunities for students to apply lecture content to a diverse range of genetic disease and population scenarios.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 7 To Week 12(07 Sep - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Animal Breeding Programs

This lecture series explores the principles of selective breeding to improve animal production and to reduce the incidence of genetic diseases.
learning_activities.topic_description: Animal Breeding Programs This lecture series explores the principles of selective breeding to improve animal production and to reduce the incidence of genetic diseases.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 7 To Week 12(07 Sep - 25 Oct)
learning_activities.topic:
Animal Breeding Tutorials

A series of small group tutorials and larger group calculation practice sessions will provide opportunities for students to apply lecture content to animal breeding scenarios.
learning_activities.topic_description: Animal Breeding Tutorials A series of small group tutorials and larger group calculation practice sessions will provide opportunities for students to apply lecture content to animal breeding scenarios.

learning_activities.topic:
Semester Review

Week 13 will provide review lectures and tutorials of the entire subject and offer an opportunity to practice exam questions
learning_activities.topic_description: Semester Review Week 13 will provide review lectures and tutorials of the entire subject and offer an opportunity to practice exam questions
